doom.driver: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops.
doom.summary: Day 29 of the Iran war and it's now formally a regional affair — Iranian missiles are landing on Saudi soil and hitting U.S. service members, which is precisely the kind of escalation that turns 'air campaign' into 'ground war.' Meanwhile Rubio says it'll be over in 'weeks, not months,' which is exactly what people said about Afghanistan circa 2001. The MAGA civil war over whether this was a good idea at all, combined with a government shutdown so long TSA agents haven't been paid in a month, suggests the home front is not exactly rock solid either.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran striking a Saudi airbase and wounding U.S. troops marks geographic expansion of the conflict beyond Iran's borders into Gulf partner territory.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: The US government shutdown is now the longest in history, TSA agents are unpaid for 35+ days, and Congress left for Easter recess without resolution.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: The Guardian's headline that AI 'got the blame for the Iran school bombing — but the truth is more worrying' hints at opaque targeting processes in active wartime.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: GitHub's quiet opt-in of private repos for Copilot training and OpenAI's Codex plugin expansion are routine capability creep, not acute threats.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Iran-linked hackers breaching FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email mid-war is a cyber escalation signal that retaliatory digital operations are active.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, interior scene — we are inside a hardened aircraft shelter at Prince Sultan Air Base, looking outward through the massive blast doors that stand partially open, framing the devastation outside like a dark mouth opening onto hell. The shelter's interior is deep charcoal shadow, rough reinforced concrete walls cracked and dusted with debris, a single overhead emergency light casting dim crimson-orange glow across the curved bunker ceiling. Through the gap of the blast doors, the outside world is visible in brutal detail — the sun-bleached Najd plateau stretching flat to the horizon under a pale amber-tinted sky choked with dust haze, the air milky and oppressive. On the concrete apron outside, a blackened blast crater dominates the tarmac, its scorched edges radiating shrapnel debris and crumpled aluminum aircraft skin panels across a wide radius. A damaged F-15E sits on the flight line with its tail section twisted, paint burned to bare metal, one wing crumpled downward. A boxy yellow-red crash rescue vehicle sprays white foam arcs across smoldering wreckage. In the middle ground, two figures in OCP camouflage uniforms half-carry a third figure hunched between them, moving across the devastated apron toward shelter. The color palette is dark crimson and charcoal throughout, firelight reflections dancing on the concrete blast door interior, high contrast between the sheltered darkness and the harsh amber ruin outside. No antialiasing, no text.

dawn.driver: Scientists film whale giving birth while other whales work together to help her. Female named Rounder surrounded by family members when about to give birth to her second calf
dawn.blurb: WHALES FORM FAMILY TO WELCOME NEW LIFE
dawn.summary: In a world consumed by war headlines, scientists captured something quietly extraordinary: a sperm whale birth attended by a circle of protective female relatives — cooperation, care, and new life all in one frame. The footage of 'Rounder' and her pod offers a rare window into animal society that mirrors the best of our own. On a day when cooperation between humans feels scarce, the ocean reminded us it never went away.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Filming a live sperm whale birth is a first-of-its-kind scientific achievement, expanding our understanding of cetacean social behavior and reproduction.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: The whale pod's coordinated protective behavior around Rounder is a vivid, documented example of non-human cooperative care — a concept that transcends species.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: A successful sperm whale birth is meaningful conservation news for a species still recovering from historic whaling; every calf counts.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: The Artemis II crew arriving at Kennedy Space Center signals that humans are still reaching for the stars even amid geopolitical turbulence on Earth.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Europeans building home mini solar farms in growing numbers represents a grassroots energy resilience movement quietly spreading across the continent.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Wide aerial view looking down at a 45-degree angle, vast open Caribbean Sea off Dominica dominates the frame — deep luminous blue-green tropical water saturated with rich gold afternoon sunlight breaking through the surface in long diagonal shafts, the volcanic emerald mountains of Dominica rising faintly at the horizon edge, warm amber and jade tones flooding the scene from above. Far below the aerial vantage, a near-perfect circular formation of eleven massive dark charcoal-gray sperm whales arranged like a living flower around a central point, their enormous wrinkled bodies radiating outward from the mother Rounder at the center, some adults visibly rolled belly-up showing white undersides, broad foreheads pressed inward and upward. A tiny 4-meter newborn calf visible tail-first at the circle's heart, glistening blue-gray, surrounded and dwarfed by the adult females lifting it toward the glittering gold-lit surface. A small white research vessel sits at the scene's edge, miniature figures leaning over the rail. A tiny drone hangs high above, a dark speck against the warm sky. Rippling circular sound-wave patterns radiate outward through the water from the pod. Rich gold and vibrant emerald color grade, saturated and alive, afternoon sun shimmering across the vast ocean surface.
